mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate over the prior month signals easier discounting and lower funding stress, which lifts equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are negative or below baseline in more than 60% of months when FEDFUNDS falls month-over-month, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above a mild recession threshold indicates deteriorating labor income and weaker demand, which should precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes with UNRATE above 6.5% generate positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 40% of cases, or if the sign is not negative in a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in RSAFS and HOUST indicates broadening household and retail demand, which supports near-term revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint signal from RSAFS and HOUST does not outperform either series alone in walk-forward tests, or if triggered co-acceleration episodes fail to deliver positive 20-day returns more than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in INDPRO signals weakening industrial output and tightening real activity, which should pressure earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if three-month industrial production declines are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 50% of cases, or if the rolling 3-year rank correlation is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is elevated while GDPC1 growth is weak, real consumption power erodes and equity multiples compress, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while GDPC1 is flat or falling
failure_condition: Reject if high-inflation, weak-growth episodes do not produce negative 20-day SPX returns more often than 60% of the time, or if the interaction term is not negative in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A continued decline in PAYEMS after a labor-market peak signals slowing payroll growth and weakening income formation, which should lead S&P 500 returns lower over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if payroll declines are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than half of triggered cases, or if no negative edge appears in any 4-quarter rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, a high FEDFUNDS rate combined with rising CPIAUCSL keeps real policy restrictive and compresses equity valuations, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate and rising-inflation periods do not underperform baseline over 20 trading days, or if the combined signal is not more negative than either proxy alone across rolling evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in HOUST after a prior trough signals recovering housing demand and wealth effects, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days in early recovery regimes.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises month-over-month after a prior 3-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if housing rebound triggers fail to produce positive 5-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the edge disappears after controlling for the broader market trend.
